}

#include <sstream>
#include <iomanip>
#include <cctype>

class Solution {
public:
    string discountPrices(string sentence, int discount) {
        // Split into words
        vector<string> words;
        istringstream iss(sentence);
        string word;
        while (iss >> word) {
            words.push_back(word);
        }
        
        // Process each word
        for (string& w : words) {
            if (w[0] == '$') {
                string digits_part = w.substr(1);
                // Check if the rest are all digits
                bool all_digits = true;
                for (char c : digits_part) {
                    if (!isdigit(c)) {
                        all_digits = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (all_digits) {
                    // Valid price, process
                    long long original = stoll(digits_part);
                    double discounted = original * (100.0 - discount) / 100.0;
                    ostringstream oss;
                    oss << fixed << setprecision(2) << discounted;
                    string new_price = "$" + oss.str();
                    w = new_price;
                }
            }
        }
        
        // Join words back
        ostringstream result_ss;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < words.size(); ++i) {
            if (i > 0) result_ss << " ";
            result_ss << words[i];
        }
        return result_ss.str();
    }
};

But for example, if N is 100 cents (i.e., $1), discount is 50%, then new price is 100 * 50 / 100 = 50 cents тЖТ $0.50. If discount is 30%, then 100 * 70 / 100 = 70 тЖТ $0.70. But if N is 10 cents and discount is 30%, then 10 * 70 / 100 = 7 cents тЖТ $0.07.

But what if N*(100 - D) is not divisible by 100? For example, N=1, D=10 тЖТ 1 * 90 / 100 = 0.9 тЖТ $0.90. But if N= 13, D= 25 тЖТ 13 * 75 = 975 тЖТ divided by 100 тЖТ 9.75 тЖТ $9.75. So using integer arithmetic here would require handling fractions. But perhaps using doubles is easier.

But given that the code uses fixed and setprecision(2), perhaps the double approach is sufficient.

Now, time and space complexity.

solution: 
```cpp
#include <sstream>
#include <iomanip>
#include <cctype>

class Solution {
public:
    string discountPrices(string sentence, int discount) {
        std::vector<std::string> words;
        std::istringstream iss(sentence);
        std::string word;
        while (iss >> word) {
            words.push_back(word);
        }
        
        for (std::string& w : words) {
            if (w[0] == '$') {
                std::string digits_part = w.substr(1);
                if (digits_part.empty()) {
                    continue;
                }
                bool all_digits = true;
                for (char c : digits_part) {
                    if (!std::isdigit(c)) {
                        all_digits = false;
                        break;
                    }
                }
                if (all_digits) {
                    long long original = std::stoll(digits_part);
                    double discounted = original * (100.0 - discount) / 100.0;
                    std::ostringstream oss;
                    oss << std::fixed << std::setprecision(2) << discounted;
                    w = '$' + oss.str();
                }
            }
        }
        
        std::ostringstream result_ss;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < words.size(); ++i) {
            if (i > 0) result_ss << " ";
            result_ss << words[i];
        }
        return result_ss.str();
    }
};
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution splits the sentence into words and processes each word to check if it represents a valid price. Valid prices are identified by starting with '$' followed by digits. For valid prices, the discount is applied, and the result is formatted to two decimal places. The algorithm efficiently processes each word in linear time relative to the input size, ensuring optimal performance.
